perturbation.original_text: If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with discussions or negotiations relating to a Transaction with the other party and the party so deciding promptly informs the other party of that decision, or at any time upon the request of TBI for any reason, EnCana will promptly deliver to TBI or, at the option of EnCana, destroy all written (and electronic) Evaluation Material. In the event of such a decision or request, all other Evaluation Material prepared by EnCana shall be destroyed, and in no event shall EnCana be obligated to disclose or provide the Evaluation Material prepared by it or its Representatives to TBI provided that a single copy of each item returned or destroyed may be retained in the files of EnCana's outside legal counsel for the purpose of resolving any disputes that may arise under this letter agreement. If requested, the destruction of Evaluation Material shall be certified in writing by an authorized officer of EnCana. Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with discussions or negotiations relating to a Transaction with the other party and the party so deciding promptly informs the other party of that decision, or at any time upon the request of TBI for any reason, EnCana will promptly deliver to TBI or, at the option of EnCana, destroy all written (and electronic) Evaluation Material. In the event of such a decision or request, all other Evaluation Material prepared by EnCana shall be destroyed. If requested, the destruction of Evaluation Material shall be certified in writing by an authorized officer of EnCana. Notwithstanding the termination of any discussions or the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality hereunder for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The clause stating 'in no event shall EnCana be obligated to disclose or provide the Evaluation Material prepared by it or its Representatives to TBI provided that a single copy of each item returned or destroyed may be retained in the files of EnCana's outside legal counsel for the purpose of resolving any disputes that may arise under this letter agreement' is removed. This omission creates uncertainty, especially if disputes arise. This omission directly impacts Section 9(d), which states that TBI makes no warranty as to the completeness of the Evaluation Material and has no liability for its use. This contradiction makes it unclear if EnCana is obligated to provide the retained Evaluation Material to TBI if a dispute arises regarding the completeness or accuracy of the initial Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) EnCana understands and agrees that neither TBI nor any of its Representatives has made or makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material or shall have any liability to EnCana or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors in or omissions therefrom, except in each case to the extent expressly provided in any definitive agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed EnCana to retain a copy of the Evaluation Material for dispute resolution while also stating that EnCana was not obligated to disclose or provide the Evaluation Material to TBI. The changed text removes the clause stating that EnCana is not obligated to disclose the Evaluation Material, thereby creating uncertainty about EnCana's obligation to provide the retained evaluation material to TBI if a dispute arises. The contradicted text, Section 9(d), states that TBI makes no warranty as to the completeness of the Evaluation Material and has no liability for its use. By removing the protection that EnCana is not obligated to disclose, it implies that EnCana might be obligated to disclose this material in a dispute, which contradicts TBI's disclaimer of liability in Section 9(d) regarding the completeness or accuracy of the material. The omission makes the contract ambiguous, leading to a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Standstill Agreement. For a period of 18 months from the date hereof, EnCana agrees that it and its affiliates shall not, without the prior written request of TBI, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, or otherwise, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of more than 1% of any class of voting securities issued by TBI or any material assets of TBI or any securities or material assets of any subsidiary of TBI, (b) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (c) make, or in any way participate in, any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission) with respect to the securities of TBI, or advise or seek to influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, or giving of consents with respect to, any securities of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (d) seek or propose to influence or control (whether though a "group," as such term is used in Rule 13d-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 or otherwise) the management, board of directors, policies or affairs of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (e) to the extent doing so would require the public disclosure of such action by TBI, make any request to waive or amend any provision of this Section 5, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with any of the foregoing or (g) encourage any third party to do any of the foregoing; provided, however, that this Section 5 shall terminate with respect to EnCana if a person or entity (other than EnCana or an affiliate of EnCana) acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, directly or indirectly, by merger or otherwise, more than 50% of the voting securities of TBI (i.e., those securities of TBI that are entitled to participate in the annual election of directors), or otherwise acquires, enters an agreement to acquire, or publicly proposes to acquire, the ability to control the management or policies of TBI. As used in this Section 5, the term "securities" shall mean any securities of TBI and any direct or indirect warrants, rights or options to acquire securities of TBI.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Standstill Agreement. For a period of 18 months from the date hereof, EnCana agrees that it and its affiliates shall not, without the prior written request of TBI, directly or indirectly, alone or in concert with others, (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, by purchase, or otherwise, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of more than 1% of any class of voting securities issued by TBI or any material assets of TBI or any securities or material assets of any subsidiary of TBI, (b) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (c) make, or in any way participate in, any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission) with respect to the securities of TBI, or advise or seek to influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of, or giving of consents with respect to, any securities of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (d) seek or propose to influence or control (whether though a "group," as such term is used in Rule 13d-5 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 or otherwise) the management, board of directors, policies or affairs of TBI or any of its subsidiaries, (e) to the extent doing so would require the public disclosure of such action by TBI, make any request to waive or amend any provision of this Section 5, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with any of the foregoing or (g) encourage any third party to do any of the foregoing. As used in this Section 5, the term "securities" shall mean any securities of TBI and any direct or indirect warrants, rights or options to acquire securities of TBI.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that provides termination of the Standstill Agreement if a third party acquires more than 50% of TBI's voting securities, Section 5 becomes internally contradictory. It maintains the restrictions on EnCana's actions for 18 months, even if TBI's ownership changes drastically, making the Standstill Agreement potentially irrelevant or unfairly restrictive. This creates a contradiction as the initial purpose of the Standstill Agreement was to protect TBI during negotiations, but it now extends indefinitely regardless of TBI's control. This contradicts section 9 (a) which states that neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to a Transaction by virtue of this letter or any written or oral expression with respect to such a Transaction by any of its Representatives except for the matters specifically agreed to in this letter agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Unless and until a definitive agreement between the parties with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither TBI nor EnCana will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to a Transaction by virtue of this letter or any written or oral expression with respect to such a Transaction by any of its Representatives except for the matters specifically agreed to in this letter agreement. Each party further agrees that neither party shall have any obligation to authorize or pursue with the other party any Transaction. Each party acknowledges and agrees that each reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, to reject any and all proposals and to terminate discussions and negotiations with the other at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a termination clause for the Standstill Agreement if a third party acquires more than 50% of TBI's voting securities. The changed text omits this clause. The explanation states this omission creates a contradiction with Section 9(a) because it extends the Standstill Agreement indefinitely regardless of TBI's control, conflicting with the principle that neither party is bound to a Transaction until a definitive agreement is in place. This creates a direct conflict, as EnCana remains restricted even if TBI's ownership changes hands, directly relating to a transaction.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise agreed, all (i) communications regarding a possible Transaction, (ii) requests for information and (iii) discussions or questions regarding procedures, will be submitted or directed to the respective party's Chief Executive Officer, Chief Financial Officer or General Counsel.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise agreed, all (i) communications regarding a possible Transaction, (ii) requests for information and (iii) discussions or questions regarding procedures, will be submitted or directed to the respective party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Chief Executive Officer, Chief Financial Officer or General Counsel.' in Section 6, it creates a vague and undefined communication protocol, implying that any employee of the respective party can be contacted. This is in direct contradiction with Section 1, which defines 'Representatives' as a specific group of people including affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, lenders or advisers and representatives of the foregoing. By not having a clear contact list, this introduces ambiguity and potential for unauthorized individuals to engage in communications related to the transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representatives " shall mean such party's affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, lenders or advisers and representatives of the foregoing. EnCana recognizes and acknowledges the potential competitive value of the Evaluation Material and the damage that could result from the disclosure thereof to third parties. Accordingly, EnCana agrees that the Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and related actions, and that such information will be kept confidential by EnCana and its Representatives; provided, however, that (w) such information may be disclosed by EnCana to its Representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction or their participation therein (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed of the confidential nature of the information), (x) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement if any disclosure of such information is made with TBI's prior written consent, (y) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that EnCana believes, based on the advice of counsel, that it is legally required to disclose such information in order to avoid committing a violation of any law, rule or regulation, including any rules or regulations of any securities association, stock exchange or national securities quotation system, provided that EnCana Ex. (e)(3)-1 provides prompt notice to TBI of the proposed disclosure and takes the other actions required in connection with a required disclosure pursuant to Section 3 below and (z) it shall not constitute a breach of this letter agreement for EnCana or its Representatives to disclose such information to the extent that such disclosure is permitted pursuant to Section 3 below. EnCana agrees to be responsible for any breach of this letter agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that communications regarding the transaction should be directed to specific high-level officers (CEO, CFO, or General Counsel). The changed text removes this specificity, stating that communications can be directed to the 'respective party' without further qualification. The contradicted text defines 'Representatives' as affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, lenders, or advisers. By removing the specification in the changed text, it implies that any employee can be contacted, which expands the scope of who can be involved in communications beyond the specifically defined 'Representatives' who are authorized to handle confidential information, as outlined in the contradicted text. This creates a strong contradiction because the contradicted section places responsibility on EnCana for the actions of its Representatives, implying a controlled group, while the changed text opens up communications to potentially any employee, creating ambiguity about who is responsible for handling confidential information and potentially breaching the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Until the first anniversary of the date hereof, EnCana will not, nor will it permit any of its subsidiaries, directors, officers or employees to, directly or indirectly, solicit or hire the services, as employee, consultant or otherwise, of any employee of TBI or its subsidiaries with whom EnCana had contact in connection with its consideration of the Transaction or who became known to EnCana specifically in connection with its consideration of the Transaction, except that this provision shall not prohibit the hiring of any such employee (i) who responds to any public advertising for employment without any other direct or indirect solicitation or (ii) whose employment by TBI or its subsidiary has been terminated prior to the commencement of discussions with such employee.
perturbation.changed_text: Until the first anniversary of the date hereof, EnCana will not, nor will it permit any of its subsidiaries, directors, officers or employees to, directly or indirectly, solicit or hire the services, as employee, consultant or otherwise, of any employee of TBI or its subsidiaries with whom EnCana had contact in connection with its consideration of the Transaction or who became known to EnCana specifically in connection with its consideration of the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception that allows EnCana to hire TBI employees who respond to public advertisements, it creates a direct conflict with the implied understanding of fair market competition. The modified clause now prohibits EnCana from hiring *any* TBI employee who they had contact with during the transaction consideration, regardless of how that employee's application originated. The change now contradicts Section 9(a), where it mentions: Each party acknowledges and agrees that each reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, to reject any and all proposals and to terminate discussions and negotiations with the other at any time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Unless and until a definitive agreement between the parties with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither TBI nor EnCana will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to a Transaction by virtue of this letter or any written or oral expression with respect to such a Transaction by any of its Representatives except for the matters specifically agreed to in this letter agreement. Each party further agrees that neither party shall have any obligation to authorize or pursue with the other party any Transaction. Each party acknowledges and agrees that each reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, to reject any and all proposals and to terminate discussions and negotiations with the other at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained an exception allowing EnCana to hire TBI employees who responded to public advertisements. The changed text removes this exception, creating a direct prohibition on hiring any TBI employee with whom EnCana had contact during the transaction, regardless of the circumstances of their application. This contradicts Section 9(a), which grants each party the right to reject any and all proposals and terminate discussions, implying the freedom to engage in fair market competition, including hiring employees who apply through public channels.
